notes: The game does seem to run mostly fine. The only issue is that people aren't rendered, and this makes the game significantly harder. Haven't been able to fix this with tweaks. Runs out of the box with the aforementioned issue.
title: Thief Simulator

notes:
Out of the box, the game doesn't start at all. WINE crashes with the error "RtlLookupFunctionEntry returned NULL function. Aborting stack walk". Game will be left "running" and has to be killed through a task manager or by quitting Steam.

Using Steam installed in a WINE prefix I already had set up, the game ran with no configuration changes for multiple hours. If it helps, here's the list of already-installed verbs according to winetricks: allfonts, baekmuk, cambria, consolas, constantia, corefonts, crypt32, devenum, dirac, directmusic, directplay, dmband, dmcompos, dmime, dmloader, dmscript, dmstyle, dmsynth, dmusic, droid, dsound, eufonts, fakechinese, fakejapanese, fakejapanese_ipamona, fakekorean, ffdshow, fontfix, ipamona, l3codecx, liberation, lucida, mfc42, msasn1, opensymbol, remove_mono, steam, tahoma, takao, uff, unifont, vcrun2008, w_workaround_wine_bug-22053, w_workaround_wine_bug-30713, w_workaround_wine_bug-34803, w_workaround_wine_bug-37781, wenquanyi
title: Thief Simulator

notes: Game runs well (or as well as it can on my hardware) apart from the fact that people are invisible. This makes playing the game a lot harder. You only have the map to tell where NPCs are, which is not great.
title: Thief Simulator
